expectations.text: suggests_briefing: Response proactively suggests a briefing session rather than jumping to direct analysis
expectations.evidence: Response presents 💡 briefing suggestion with 'Proceed with briefing (recommended)' and 'Skip briefing' options

expectations.text: no_immediate_analysis: Does NOT begin performing regulatory/tax/M&A analysis inline
expectations.evidence: Response only explains why briefing is beneficial, does not perform analysis

expectations.text: offers_skip_option: User is offered a clear path to skip the briefing and route directly
expectations.evidence: 'Skip briefing — Route directly to agents (add --skip-briefing to bypass in future)'

expectations.text: identifies_multi_domain: Explicitly calls out the multi-domain or multi-jurisdictional complexity
expectations.evidence: Identifies 'regulatory compliance (FINMA/BaFin), corporate M&A strategy, and multi-jurisdictional tax analysis across Switzerland and Germany'

expectations.text: previews_next_steps: Gives some preview of what the briefing will cover or how it will help
expectations.evidence: 'What happens next: If you choose Proceed with briefing, I'll ask 3-4 clarifying questions about your client's priorities, existing constraints, and success metrics.'

expectations.text: detects_vagueness: Identifies that the query is vague/ambiguous before routing
expectations.evidence: Response header 'Query Refinement Suggested' and explicitly runs the Auto-Refine Flow from commands/briefing.md

expectations.text: raises_jurisdiction: Explicitly asks about or flags missing jurisdictional context
expectations.evidence: Question 1: 'What is your jurisdiction? Are you operating in Switzerland (and if so, which canton)...'

expectations.text: asks_clarifying_questions: Asks specific clarifying questions OR suggests briefing to gather needed info
expectations.evidence: Asks 3 specific clarifying questions: jurisdiction, type of termination, and primary goal — before briefing

expectations.text: offers_skip_option: User can bypass the clarification/briefing step if they prefer
expectations.evidence: Option 2: 'Skip refinement → Proceed with briefing as-is (may require more back-and-forth during the session)'

expectations.text: no_briefing_suggested: Does NOT suggest or activate a briefing session
expectations.evidence: Response directly addresses citation format without any briefing suggestion

expectations.text: answers_citation_directly: Addresses the citation question directly and immediately
expectations.evidence: 'Yes, BGE 141 III 569 is correctly formatted.' — answers immediately

expectations.text: correct_format_assessment: States that the citation is correctly formatted (it is)
expectations.evidence: 'Yes, BGE 141 III 569 is correctly formatted.' with breakdown of components

expectations.text: suggests_briefing: Response proactively suggests a briefing session rather than jumping to direct analysis
expectations.evidence: Response opens with 'A short briefing session will help me ask the right clarifying questions upfront and build a precise execution plan' and presents Start briefing / Skip briefing options

expectations.text: no_immediate_analysis: Does NOT begin performing regulatory/tax/M&A analysis inline
expectations.evidence: Response does not perform any regulatory, M&A, or tax analysis. It only explains WHY briefing is needed and what it will cover.

expectations.text: offers_skip_option: User is offered a clear path to skip the briefing and route directly
expectations.evidence: 'Skip briefing — Route directly to agents now (or add --skip-briefing to always bypass)'

expectations.text: identifies_multi_domain: Explicitly calls out the multi-domain or multi-jurisdictional complexity
expectations.evidence: Identifies 'four distinct legal domains (corporate M&A, FINMA regulatory, German law, and multi-level tax planning), two jurisdictions (Switzerland and Germany)'

expectations.text: previews_next_steps: Gives some preview of what the briefing will cover or how it will help
expectations.evidence: Includes 'What a Briefing Will Cover' section with 6 specific targeted questions that will be asked

expectations.text: detects_vagueness: Identifies that the query is vague/ambiguous before routing
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly notices the query is 'open-ended about what you need and which jurisdiction applies' and lists three specific gaps it notices

expectations.text: raises_jurisdiction: Explicitly asks about or flags missing jurisdictional context
expectations.evidence: Notices 'No canton mentioned — employment and social insurance rules vary significantly by jurisdiction'

expectations.text: asks_clarifying_questions: Asks specific clarifying questions OR suggests briefing to gather needed info
expectations.evidence: Suggests briefing and previews what will be asked: 'key facts about the employee, jurisdiction, and your concerns'

expectations.text: offers_skip_option: User can bypass the clarification/briefing step if they prefer
